How (device steps): Delete the app first then install the fresh build and open it
Expected result: App launches with no crash; onboarding appears

How (device steps): Tap through onboarding to the end
Expected result: Lands on Home; no stuck/blank screen

What to test: Onboarding skip when server+model already set
How (device steps): Configure a remote server + model during onboarding then tap Continue
Expected result: Routes straight into the app (Home); remaining onboarding skipped

How (device steps): Models -> pick a small text model (Qwen/Gemma) -> Download
Expected result: Progress advances -> completes -> shows Downloaded

How (device steps): After the text download completes open Models
Expected result: The model card shows a Downloaded mark; count matches what you downloaded

What to test: Model info / credibility shown on the card
How (device steps): Open Models and read a model card / open its details
Expected result: Model size, quant, and credibility/source info render (not a blank card)

How (device steps): Download a vision GGUF that has a projector (mmproj) file
Expected result: Both the main + mmproj files download; model shows Downloaded

How (device steps): While the mmproj is mid-flight read the downloads icon badge then open Download Manager
Expected result: Badge number == running + queued count in Download Manager (they agree)

How (device steps): Settings/Models -> download a whisper model (base.en or medium)
Expected result: Completes and is selectable for voice; NOT auto-loaded into memory

How (device steps): Download a second whisper size (e.g. small.en) alongside base.en
Expected result: Completes; both listed

How (device steps): Download the TTS voice model (Kokoro)
Expected result: Completes and is usable for spoken replies

How (device steps): Download an image model (e.g. AnythingV5 / Absolute Reality)
Expected result: Completes; zip extraction finishes before it shows usable

How (device steps): Download the biggest text model your device can hold
Expected result: Completes

How (device steps): Download a .litertlm model (e.g. gemma litert) - Android only
Expected result: Completes; selectable

What to test: Delete does not cancel another download
How (device steps): Start download A (in-flight) -> delete a different downloaded model B
Expected result: A keeps downloading; deleting B does not cancel A

How (device steps): Start several downloads at once
Expected result: Some run concurrently and the rest queue; queue drains in order; no drop

How (device steps): Turn on airplane mode -> start a new download
Expected result: A clear no-network / connection error; NO phantom stuck-at-0 entry; retriable when network returns

How (device steps): Start a NEW download -> force-kill the app mid-download -> reopen -> Download Manager
Expected result: A retriable/failed entry is shown after a full cold relaunch; NO phantom/stuck entry

What to test: Truncated file not listed as ready
How (device steps): If a download is cut short (below size floor) check the model list
Expected result: The truncated file is NOT listed as a completed/loadable model

How (device steps): Force-kill during an image model zip extraction -> reopen
Expected result: On next launch it re-extracts or shows a retriable failed card; never a half-ready model

How (device steps): On an image model whose extraction failed -> tap Retry in Download Manager
Expected result: It re-downloads / re-extracts (no Download not found dead-end)

What to test: Multi-file image download survives app resume
How (device steps): Start a HuggingFace/CoreML (multi-file) image model download; background the app mid-download; reopen
Expected result: Card still shows downloading/progress; it does NOT flip to failed/Retry.

What to test: Zip image unzip survives app resume
How (device steps): Download an SD/zip image model; during the unzip window, background and reopen the app
Expected result: No "failed" flash during processing; ends in success (model installed).

What to test: Partial re-unzip is not registered as complete
How (device steps): Force-kill during a zip unzip (mnn/qnn backend), then relaunch
Expected result: Model shows as re-downloadable/failed, NOT installed; selecting it does not crash generation.

What to test: Valid model survives a transient FS error
How (device steps): With a valid multi-GB model installed, repeatedly open Models / trigger the orphan scan + storage view (under a flaky filesystem if possible)
Expected result: The installed model stays listed and loadable; never silently removed. (Mainly a code-fix + regression-test item; manual is a weak proxy.)

What to test: Download an embedding model (first KB use)
How (device steps): First KB attach will download the embedding model - do it now or allow it in phase 6
Expected result: Embedding model downloads and is available for indexing

How (device steps): Select a text GGUF model -> New chat -> type -> Send

How (device steps): Select the litert model -> New chat -> send - Android only

How (device steps): Model Settings -> Text -> Advanced -> Backend = GPU/OpenCL -> reload -> send

How (device steps): Select litert model -> Advanced -> Backend = CPU -> reload -> send - Android only

How (device steps): Backend = NPU (Beta)/HTP -> reload -> send - Android device with NPU

How (device steps): Set Temperature (Model Settings or chat modal) -> send a prompt

How (device steps): Fill the context window then keep sending
